title: Leading Large Projects: Lead Through People, Not Authority
excerpt: The capstone skill of staff-level project leadership: creating alignment through influence, trust, and making people feel heard, when you have no direct authority over the teams doing the work.

title: Leading Large Projects: Create Shared Understanding
excerpt: How to reduce complexity across teams using mental models, precise naming, diagrams, and written design documents so that everyone sees the same picture.

title: Leading Large Projects: Explore Before You Decide
excerpt: Why jumping to solutions too early is one of the most common mistakes in large projects, and how to align mental models across teams before committing to an approach.

title: Leading Large Projects: Drive the Project
excerpt: Driving a project is not about moving fast. It is about making decisions, adjusting direction, reacting to risks, and keeping everyone pointed at the destination.

title: Leading Large Projects: Add Structure
excerpt: How to convert ambiguity into clarity by defining roles, agreeing on scope and milestones, setting up logistics, and giving the project a skeleton it can grow into.

title: Leading Large Projects: Clarify the Fundamentals
excerpt: Before execution begins, make the goals, customers, success metrics, stakeholders, constraints, risks, and history explicit. Skipping this step is how you solve the wrong problem with perfect execution.

title: Leading Large Projects: Build Context with Three Maps
excerpt: Staff engineers navigate organizations, not just systems. Build three maps to understand where the project fits, how the org works, and where you are going.

title: Leading Large Projects: Align on the Why
excerpt: One of the most common failure modes in large projects is building the wrong thing really well. Align with your project sponsor on what success looks like before doing anything else.

title: Leading Large Projects: Build Your Second Brain
excerpt: Before you try to solve a large project, externalize it. Create a single document that acts as an extension of your brain for the duration of the project.

title: Leading Large Projects: Embrace the Chaos
excerpt: The beginning of a large project is supposed to feel chaotic. Feeling overwhelmed is not a signal you are failing. It is a signal you are doing staff-level work.

title: Leading Large Projects as a Staff Engineer: A Series Overview
excerpt: A 10-phase guide to leading large, ambiguous, cross-team projects as a staff engineer, drawn from The Staff Engineer's Path with a running LLM Gateway example.

title: Natural Language to SQL with TinyLlama: Four Approaches Compared
excerpt: Building a natural language to SQL system using a 1.1B parameter model, comparing template matching, direct schema-to-SQL, slim prompting, and agentic ReAct approaches with rigorous evaluations.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 13: Putting It All Together
excerpt: The capstone of the Crucial Conversations series, integrating all nine dialogue principles into a complete framework with diagnostic questions to identify where you are stuck and which skill to apply.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 12: Navigating Tough Cases
excerpt: How to apply crucial conversation skills to the hardest scenarios engineers face, including broken trust, sensitive feedback, people who refuse to talk, and speaking truth to power.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 11: Move to Action
excerpt: Why good conversations still fail to produce results, and how to use the four decision-making methods and the WWWF framework to turn shared understanding into clear commitments.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 10: Retake Your Pen
excerpt: How to build resilience when receiving tough feedback by understanding the pen metaphor, separating what was said from what you made it mean, and reclaiming control of your self-worth.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 9: Explore Others' Paths
excerpt: How to listen effectively when others go silent or blow up, using the AMPP toolkit to restore safety and help people share what they really think.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 8: STATE My Path
excerpt: How to speak up honestly without triggering defensiveness using the STATE framework: Share facts, Tell your story, Ask for others' paths, Talk tentatively, and Encourage testing.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 7: Make It Safe
excerpt: When safety breaks in a conversation, stop discussing content and rebuild safety first using Mutual Purpose, Mutual Respect, Contrasting, and the CRIB framework.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 6: Learn to Look
excerpt: How to spot the moment a conversation turns crucial and detect safety problems early by watching for Silence and Violence patterns in yourself and others.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 5: Master My Stories
excerpt: How to take control of your emotions by examining the stories you tell yourself, using the Path to Action model to separate facts from interpretation and avoid Victim, Villain, and Helpless narratives.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 4: Start With Heart
excerpt: The foundational skill of crucial conversations: managing your own motives before opening your mouth, using four transformative questions to stay focused on what you really want.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 3: Choose Your Topic
excerpt: You cannot solve the right problem if you are discussing the wrong one. Learn the CPR framework to unbundle Content, Pattern, and Relationship issues and focus on what actually matters.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 2: The Power of Dialogue
excerpt: How the free flow of meaning between people drives better engineering decisions, and why the most influential engineers reject the false choice between honesty and respect.

title: Crucial Conversations Ch. 1: What Makes a Conversation Crucial
excerpt: An introduction to crucial conversations -- the high-stakes, emotionally charged discussions that shape your career, your team, and your engineering outcomes more than any technical skill.

title: Mixed Precision Training: bfloat16, FP8, and When to Use What
excerpt: A practical guide to floating point formats in deep learning training, covering float32, float16, bfloat16, and FP8. Explains why bfloat16 is the modern default, when FP8 makes sense, and how mixed precision training works in PyTorch.

title: Chain of Density Summarization: Getting Better Summaries from LLMs
excerpt: A practical guide to Chain of Density prompting, an iterative summarization technique that produces concise, information-dense summaries by progressively packing more entities into a fixed word count across multiple rewriting rounds.

title: Speculative Decoding: Making LLM Inference Faster
excerpt: A deep dive into speculative decoding, an inference optimization technique that achieves 2-3x speedups in LLM token generation by using a small draft model to propose candidates verified in parallel by the target model, with no quality degradation.

title: Prefill vs Decode: Understanding the Two Phases of LLM Inference
excerpt: A deep dive into the two core phases of LLM inference -- prefill and decode -- covering KV caching, GPU memory breakdown, inference metrics, batching strategies, and attention variants like MHA, GQA, and MQA.

title: RNN vs. CNN vs. Autoencoder vs. Attention/Transformer
excerpt: A practical guide to various deep learning architectures, including Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Autoencoders, and Attention/Transformer models, with examples and use cases.

title: AI Engineering: RAG and Agents
excerpt: Detailed exploration of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI agents, covering definitions, workflows, tools, planning, control flows, and memory management in AI applications.

title: AI Engineering: Prompt Engineering
excerpt: An in-depth look at prompt engineering, discussing its importance in guiding AI models, the anatomy of a prompt, and strategies for effective prompt design.

title: AI Engineering by Chip Huyen: Chapter 2 Notes and Summary
excerpt: A summary of Chapter 2 from AI Engineering by Chip Huyen, covering foundation model design choices, pre-training and post-training processes, sampling methods, and the probabilistic nature of AI models.

title: AI Engineering Book by Chip Huyen: Chapter 1 High level insights
excerpt: High-level insights from Chapter 1 of AI Engineering by Chip Huyen, discussing the scaling up of AI models, the importance of data, and challenges in building AI applications.

title: Notes on paper: Large Language Models as Zero-Shot Conversational Recommenders
excerpt: A summary of research on using large language models as zero-shot conversational recommenders, exploring how LLMs can provide recommendations through natural language conversations without explicit training.

title: 13 Books I read in 2023
excerpt: A curated list of 13 books read in 2023, covering topics in technology, software engineering, machine learning, and personal development.

title: Machine Learning Engineering by Andriy Burkov: Chapter 1 Notes
excerpt: Notes from Chapter 1 of Machine Learning Engineering by Andriy Burkov, discussing the process of solving practical problems using machine learning, including data collection, model training, and different learning paradigms.

title: RecMind: Large Language Model Powered Agent for Recommendation
excerpt: A summary of the RecMind paper, discussing the use of large language models as agents for recommendation systems, including architecture, advantages, challenges, and implementation patterns.

title: java trivia
excerpt: Interesting trivia and lesser-known facts about Java programming language, covering language features, history, and quirks.

title: Coursera Week 1: Intro to Neural Networks Notes
excerpt: Notes from the first week of a Coursera course on neural networks, covering the basics of neural network structure, forward propagation, and activation functions.

title: Exploring Vector Databases
excerpt: An exploration of vector databases, their use cases, and how they enable semantic search and similarity matching for AI applications.

title: 12 Books I read in 2022
excerpt: A list of 12 books read in 2022, including technical books on software engineering, machine learning, and system design.

title: Recognizing Toxic workplaces(Opinion)
excerpt: Personal insights and observations on recognizing toxic work environments, including red flags and strategies for maintaining professional well-being.

excerpt: Practical guidelines and best practices for building production-ready machine learning applications, covering data, model development, deployment, and monitoring.

title: How to be a Better Backend Engineer
excerpt: Insights and advice on becoming a better backend engineer, covering system design, scalability, performance optimization, and best practices.

excerpt: A list of 10 books read in 2020, including technical books on programming, system design, and software engineering practices.

title: Writing your own Web server that can handle multiple connections
excerpt: A guide to building a web server from scratch that can handle multiple concurrent connections, covering networking, threading, and async programming concepts.

title: ADHD Management The hard way as a software Engineer
excerpt: Personal experiences and strategies for managing ADHD as a software engineer, including productivity techniques, focus management, and work organization methods.

title: How to do large feature development?
excerpt: Strategies and approaches for managing large feature development in software engineering, including planning, breaking down work, and execution.

title: LRU Caches in Python
excerpt: An explanation of LRU (Least Recently Used) cache implementation in Python, including the functools.lru_cache decorator and custom implementations.

title: Emacs (spacemacs) Keybindings I use
excerpt: A personal guide to Emacs (Spacemacs) keybindings, covering various commands and shortcuts for efficient text editing and file management.

title: My favorite Chrome browser extensions for productivity
excerpt: A curated list of Chrome browser extensions that enhance productivity for software engineers and developers, including tools for coding, research, and workflow optimization.

title: Identifying priorities in software engineering organizations
excerpt: Strategies and frameworks for identifying and managing priorities in software engineering organizations, including techniques for balancing technical debt, features, and business needs.

title: Implementing 2d Arrays in CPP
excerpt: A guide to implementing and working with 2D arrays in C++, covering memory layout, dynamic allocation, and best practices for multi-dimensional arrays.

title: Thread-safe queue implementation in C++
excerpt: An implementation guide for thread-safe queues in C++, covering synchronization primitives, mutexes, condition variables, and concurrent programming patterns.

excerpt: A custom implementation of a string class in C++, demonstrating memory management, copy semantics, move semantics, and operator overloading.

title: Compile-time vs run time size computation for array in C++
excerpt: An exploration of compile-time versus runtime size computation for arrays in C++, covering template metaprogramming, constexpr, and array size determination techniques.

title: Topological Sort: All Possible Orders
excerpt: An explanation of topological sorting, including how to find all possible variations of task ordering given a set of prerequisites, with examples and complexity analysis.

excerpt: A comprehensive guide to measuring and evaluating machine learning model performance, covering various metrics, validation techniques, and best practices.

title: Median absolute deviation (MAD) of Errors
excerpt: An explanation of Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) as a robust measure of error in machine learning models, including its advantages over mean-based metrics.

excerpt: An exploration of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP), one of the SOLID principles, with examples demonstrating how derived classes should be substitutable for their base classes.

excerpt: An explanation of R-squared (coefficient of determination), a statistical measure used to evaluate the goodness of fit in regression models.

excerpt: A discussion of different error functions and their distributions in machine learning, covering when to use each type and their properties.

excerpt: An explanation of logarithmic loss (cross-entropy) as a loss function for classification problems, including its mathematical foundation and use cases.

excerpt: A guide to understanding ROC curves, including how to interpret them, calculate AUC, and use them for model evaluation in binary classification.

excerpt: An explanation of confusion matrices for evaluating classification models, covering true positives, false positives, true negatives, and false negatives.

excerpt: An introduction to hypothesis testing in statistics, covering null and alternative hypotheses, p-values, significance levels, and statistical inference.
